name: Unilever
cat: Consumer Goods / Beauty, Personal Care, Home Care & Foods

political.s: Unilever does not have a political action committee and does not make direct or indirect contributions to political parties or candidates. The company engages in lobbying through trade associations and spent $610,000 on direct lobbying in 2024. Employee contributions from individuals are permitted on a personal capacity basis.

charity.s: Unilever established the Unilever Foundation partnering with major NGOs including Oxfam, Save the Children, and UNICEF. The company has committed over $30 million to UNICEF since 2012 and pledged $120 million in product donations for COVID-19 relief. Focus areas include hygiene, sanitation, nutrition, education, and gender empowerment programs globally.
charity.amt: $30+ million to UNICEF since 2012; $120 million COVID-19 product donations
charity.focus: Hygiene and sanitation, nutrition, clean water, education, gender empowerment, child welfare

environment.s: Unilever has made significant sustainability commitments including net-zero emissions by 2039 and reducing plastic packaging. However, the company faces criticism for greenwashing: it produced 53 billion unrecyclable plastic sachets in 2023 (up 40% since 2010) and has scaled back some sustainability targets under investor pressure. Environmental performance is mixed with strong goals but inconsistent implementation.

labor.s: Unilever collaborates with Oxfam on labor standards and has made commitments to fair wages and working conditions across its supply chain. However, an Oxfam study found that even in Unilever's own Vietnamese factory, some workers needed overtime to earn a living wage. The company shows commitment to improvement but challenges remain in supplier management and wage adequacy.

dei.s: Unilever has implemented a comprehensive Equity, Diversity & Inclusion strategy focusing on gender equity, racial and ethnic representation, LGBTQI+ inclusion, and disability representation. The company established Regional Inclusion Councils and reported improved engagement scores and minority employee retention. Senior leadership actively sponsors and measures DEI progress.

animals.s: Unilever supports a global ban on animal testing for cosmetics and is a founding member of the International Collaboration on Cosmetics Safety. The company has developed non-animal testing alternatives since the 1980s and has multiple PETA-Approved brands including Dove. Unilever actively advocates against animal testing in regulatory forums.

guns.s: Unilever is a consumer goods company focused on beauty, personal care, home care, and foods. The company does not manufacture or sell firearms. This category is not applicable.

privacy.s: Unilever has established privacy policies including Five Privacy Fundamentals that emphasize transparency, consent, and data protection. The company complies with CCPA and other data protection regulations. It maintains a Chief Privacy Officer-led team and conducts external third-party audits of compliance.

execPay.s: CEO Fernando Fernández received €5.6 million in 2025 (partial year as CFO then CEO). For 2026, the company proposed increasing CEO compensation opportunity to approximately €18 million base performance threshold, potentially exceeding €26 million with stock price appreciation. Executive pay is benchmarked to global peer median and justified by market competitiveness.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
